about.title: A studio built on focus and community
about.intro1: Gioia Reformer Studio opened in March 2025. At a time when reformer pilates was already recognised as a quality and effective form of training, Pula had no studio specialising in this type of work.
about.intro2: Our vision was clear from the start: to bring reformer pilates closer to the wider community and create a space where health comes first. A place where everyone who wants to improve their health and physical wellbeing is welcome — in a safe, positive, and supportive environment.
about.intro3: We see learning as a continuous process. From the very opening of the studio, we have grown alongside our clients — professionally and personally — raising the standard of our work day by day.
about.intro4: Gioia is not made only by its trainers and founders. Gioia is made by its people — every person who walks through our doors.
about.valuesIntro: From day one, we have cultivated core principles that define our approach.
about.manifesto01Text: We work in small groups with a maximum of 6 reformers. This format allows us to give our full attention to every client, observe each person's technique precisely, and correct movement in real time.
about.manifesto02Text: We track every client's progress systematically. We give equal importance to small and large steps forward, because it is consistency that leads to lasting results.
about.manifesto03Text: We build relationships grounded in mutual trust and respect. Every client in our studio is heard and valued, and we actively take on feedback because we believe in open communication.
about.manifesto04Text: The energy and atmosphere in the studio matter to us as much as the quality of training. We create a space where everyone feels safe, supported, and part of a community.

classes.forWho1Text: A safe, guided introduction to movement with expert instruction.
classes.forWho2Text: Targeted work to reduce tension in the back, neck, and shoulders.
classes.forWho3Text: Deepening control, strength, and body awareness.
classes.forWho4Text: A calm yet intense workout that connects body and mind.
classes.trainingTitle: Three concepts. One goal. A strong and mindful body.
classes.trainingIntro: All sessions are 55 minutes and include a structured warm-up, the main workout, and a cool-down stretch.
classes.trainingIntro2: The reformer is our foundation, but we regularly incorporate props including dumbbells, ankle weights, a pilates ball, ring, resistance bands, and a bar.
classes.trainingIntro3: Every session works the whole body, with a particular focus on building core strength, centre stability, and movement control.
classes.trainingIntro4: You'll need non-slip socks, a towel, and water.
classes.strengthDesc: Our signature class. It combines strength exercises with classic reformer work. The session is challenging but adaptable and achievable with expert guidance. Using additional props, we further develop strength and stability. The goal is to build muscle with a focus on the core — the powerhouse — improving posture and stability. The result is a stronger body, better alignment, and more efficient movement.
classes.stretchDesc: A gentler session for beginners, those new to the reformer, those returning after a break, or anyone with mild spinal concerns. The focus is on traditional reformer pilates following the principles of Joseph Pilates, with deep strengthening and controlled stretching. The goal is to strengthen the spine, improve body alignment, and develop movement awareness in a calm, controlled way.
classes.cardioDesc: A dynamic, fast-paced session for more experienced participants. Using a jumping board — an attachment that turns the reformer into a small trampoline — the session gains a high-intensity cardio element with a focus on the lower body, with an added sense of fun and energy. Recommended for clients who already have experience on the reformer or are in good physical condition.
classes.beginnerDesc1: This session is ideal for anyone just beginning their Pilates journey, as well as those with mild spinal issues or who spend long hours sitting. The focus is on correct technique, mindful breathing, and movement control — without sudden loading or excessive intensity.
classes.beginnerDesc2: Working at a slower pace, we strengthen the deep core muscles, stabilise the spine, and improve mobility throughout the whole body. Exercises are adapted to each participant, with an emphasis on safety, postural correction, and gradual strength building.
classes.beginnerDesc3: The session is gentle but effective — helping to reduce tension in the back, improve posture, and leave the body feeling lighter.
classes.pricingNote: Depending on the type of training and how often you'd like to come, we offer a range of packages and memberships. We're happy to help you find the option that best suits your goals and schedule.
classes.individualSub: Personalised attention, complete focus on you
classes.groupSub: Up to 6 participants: group energy with personal attention

team.title: The trainers guiding your transformation
team.intro: We believe the relationship between trainer and client is one of the most important elements of progress. Everyone in our studio receives clear guidance, attention, and a sense of safety — without rush, comparison, or pressure.
team.sanjaP1: Gioia Studio is a reflection of my values and my relationship with movement. It was created to offer confidence and support — physical and personal — to everyone who wants more: strength, calm, and a positive environment. A space where you can focus entirely on yourself and leave feeling recharged.
team.sanjaP2: As a long-time lover of pilates and training, movement for me is an expression of respect for the body. The body is strong and powerful, but it needs attention and care. Just as a plant cannot survive without tending, the body cannot thrive without conscious investment.
team.sanjaP3: "Your body is your home — take care of it."
team.sanjaP4: I first encountered pilates almost twenty years ago. Through years of practice I came to understand how much small, controlled movements can bring about great change. To slow down is to take control. By connecting breath and movement, we create balance between body and mind — and in those 55 minutes, you have the chance for complete connection with yourself.
team.sanjaP5: From the very beginning it was important to me to create a studio that would be more than just another place to train — a space of shared growth and support. I am proud and grateful that clients have recognised that.
team.teaP1: My path brought me to Pula through a degree in Japanese studies, and various forms of training had been part of my life for years. My first encounter with the reformer was at Sanja's studio, where I started as a client and trained from the very beginning. That experience — from the participant's perspective — further motivated me to pursue pilates professionally.
team.teaP2: Movement is my way of finding balance, and pilates has become my tool for stability and presence. Through years of practice I developed an understanding of how the body responds to movement and what it means to train with real awareness. I deepened that knowledge through training under Asja Petersen, founder of the first pilates studio in Croatia.
team.teaP3: In sessions, my priority is for clients to feel safe, present, and stronger than when they arrived.
team.teaP4: Being a pilates instructor is the natural continuation of everything I value — self-care, continuous learning, and personal growth.
team.teamQuote: Whatever your reason for coming, at Gioia Studio we start from where you are and guide you towards the goal you want to achieve.

reviews.title: What our clients say
reviews.t1: Both trainers are wonderfully skilled and calming. The exercises are clearly explained and every movement feels intentional, and I feel so much more confident in my body.
reviews.t2: Beautiful studio, an intimate atmosphere, and genuinely attentive instruction. After just a few sessions I feel so much less tension in my back.
reviews.t3: I love that groups are small and that someone actually corrects you when needed. I leave every session feeling stronger and calmer.
reviews.t4: The space is beautiful and peaceful, and the sessions are challenging in the best way. Perfect after a long day at work.

press.title: In the Press
press.istarskiDesc: A full feature on the studio's founder and concept, from life in Vienna and Melbourne to creating an intimate reformer studio in Pula.
press.stylishDesc: The studio is highlighted as a recommended place to work out in Pula, noted for its welcoming atmosphere and personal approach.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.pageSubtitle: A glimpse inside our studio. Natural light, a calm atmosphere, and moments of focused movement.

contact.addressNote: The studio is located in the peaceful Verudela neighbourhood, just a few minutes' walk from the sea. Easy to reach by car, with free parking nearby.
